_note: Layer-2 templated. {{...}} are filled from TaskContext so evidence references real symbols/targets and downstream graph+spawn logic runs. Falls back to the literal defaults shown if a placeholder is unavailable.

findings.title: Stack buffer overflow in {{function|cgi_handler}}()
findings.summary: Unbounded copy of attacker-controlled input into a fixed stack buffer in {{function|cgi_handler}} allows overwriting the saved return address.
findings.reasoning: The decompiled body copies the request 'token' field into a 1040-byte stack buffer using {{sink|strcpy}} with no length check. The source is network-reachable and pre-auth. No stack canary is present per recon mitigation flags, so the saved return address is controllable; this is very likely exploitable via ROP.

suggested_followups.label: Sweep sibling targets for the same {{sink|strcpy}} sink
